reasoning: Based on historical base rates, the probability of a new coronavirus pandemic declared by the WHO in any given year is highly unlikely (around 1% or less). The discovery of a novel bat coronavirus with potential to infect humans is a common occurrence in virology research and frequently generates sensational news articles, but very rarely translates into an actual outbreak, let alone a global pandemic within the same year. Given the strict criteria of a WHO pandemic declaration for a new COVID-like virus before the end of 2025, and no mention of active human outbreaks or human-to-human transmission, the likelihood remains at the extreme lower bound.
